title: editing '/usr/share/containers/libpod.conf' not honored
body:
I know we moved to this approach where our downstream packages ship a '/usr/share/containers/libpod.conf' ... and i believe we want users to copy that to /etc/containers/libpod.conf and make local edits.  But it appears that changes '/usr/share/containers/libpod.conf' is not being honored.

is that by intent or a bug?

comments.body: If we are not honoring changes, that means we're not loading the file. That's a pretty fundamental problem - means we're probably running on compiled in defaults, as opposed to using the config file at all.
i agree, but I want @rhatdan do agree samewise.
/etc/containers/libpod.conf should override /usr/share/containers/libpod.conf.  If it does not, we should fix this before we ship podman 1.5.
@rhatdan I think you misunderstand ... I'm asking if I edit /usr/share/containers/libpod.conf in place, should podman honor those changes?
If we are not "honoring the changes" and there is no `/etc/containers/libpod.conf` in place - that's 100% a bug.
@baude Correct `rootful` should honor anything in /usr/share/containers/libpod.conf as long as their is no /etc/containers/libpod.conf.
ok, it is a bug as @mheon  says
